meta.subdomain: intermittent signal failures on a commuter rail segment

visible.story: The Northbridge-to-Felson commuter line has a six-kilometer segment that snakes through an old chalk cutting before climbing onto a viaduct. For three months, dispatchers have logged sporadic 'track occupied' false alarms on Block 14, always inside the cutting, mostly on damp mornings but sometimes on clear afternoons. Trains get held at red signals for no visible reason, then the block clears itself after two to nine minutes. Maintenance has walked the rails twice and found nothing: no broken bonds, no cracked insulators, no debris on the rail head. The signalling team suspects the aging jointed rail; the permanent way team blames leaf mulch from the beech trees overhanging the cutting; the electrical team wants to replace the relay racks at Felson Junction, which are nearing end of life. A junior engineer noted in passing that the cutting's original French drains were last cleared in 2009 and that the stone ballast there 'looks more like soil than rock now,' with weeds growing between sleepers. Rainfall this spring has been above average, and the cutting walls weep visibly after storms. Meanwhile, ridership data, traction current logs, axle counter health, and a portable track-circuit voltage monitor are all available. Management wants the failures gone before the summer timetable, and they want to know what is actually causing them.
visible.question: Do not give a black-box prediction. Use the available records and targeted tests to explain what hidden story-plausible cause best accounts for the outcome pattern. Your answer should name the unobserved cause in ordinary language, give evidence from queried data, rule out plausible alternatives, state a decisive test, and recommend the next action (including its dose/level where applicable). The action names are intentionally neutral and do NOT reveal which one targets the cause; decide that from the data you collect, not from the labels.

observed_variables.name: AntecedentRainfall72h
observed_variables.description: rolling 72-hour rainfall over the cutting catchment

scale.higher: more rain accumulated upstream of the segment

observed_variables.name: LeafLitterIndexOnRail
observed_variables.description: camera-estimated leaf and organic debris coverage along the rail head in Block 14

scale.higher: more leaf and organic litter on the rails

observed_variables.name: AmbientHumidityIndex
observed_variables.description: average relative humidity along the cutting during the shift

scale.higher: more humid air in the cutting

observed_variables.name: DrainServiceArrears
observed_variables.description: months since the cutting's side and French drains were last serviced

scale.higher: longer since drainage was maintained

observed_variables.name: RailToEarthLeakage
observed_variables.description: measured leakage current from rail to ground in Block 14

scale.higher: more current leaking from rail into the trackbed

observed_variables.name: SleeperBaseConductivity
observed_variables.description: electrical conductivity sampled around sleeper bases in the cutting

scale.higher: more conductive material around the sleepers

observed_variables.name: CuttingFloorPondingScore
observed_variables.description: visual ponding and saturation along the cutting floor

scale.higher: more standing water and saturated ground at track level

observed_variables.name: AverageBlockHoldMinutes
observed_variables.description: average minutes trains are held at red on Block 14 per shift

scale.higher: longer average hold times

observed_variables.name: FalseOccupancyRate
observed_variables.description: false 'track occupied' events per 100 train movements through Block 14

scale.higher: more spurious occupancy alarms

observed_variables.name: RailJointResistance
observed_variables.description: measured electrical resistance across jointed rail bonds in Block 14

scale.higher: higher (worse) resistance across rail joints

observed_variables.name: FelsonRelayAgeIndex
observed_variables.description: composite age and drift score for relay rack components at Felson Junction

scale.higher: older and more drifted relay components

observed_variables.name: AxleCounterErrorRate
observed_variables.description: diagnostic error rate from axle counter heads serving the block

scale.higher: more axle counter diagnostic errors

observed_variables.name: TrackCircuitVoltageMargin
observed_variables.description: measured voltage margin of the Block 14 track circuit relative to drop-away threshold

scale.higher: healthier voltage margin (further from spurious drop-out)

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenA
intervenable_variables.description: a scheduled civil works package applied to the cutting between Northbridge and the viaduct

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenB
intervenable_variables.description: a trackbed remediation pass performed during night possessions

intervenable_variables.name: ProtocolX
intervenable_variables.description: deploy portable instrumentation that logs continuous track-circuit and rail-to-earth measurements through the block

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenC
intervenable_variables.description: a signalling hardware refurbishment at Felson Junction

intervenable_variables.name: BlockThresholdSetpoint
intervenable_variables.description: adjust the relay drop-away sensitivity setpoint on Block 14

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenD
intervenable_variables.description: divert a share of services onto the parallel slow line during peak windows

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenE
intervenable_variables.description: a rail-head cleaning and vegetation cutback program along Block 14

intervenable_variables.name: ProtocolY
intervenable_variables.description: run an axle-counter head self-diagnostic sweep on the segment

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenF
intervenable_variables.description: apply additional rail bonding straps across existing joints in Block 14

intervenable_variables.name: RegimenG
intervenable_variables.description: repaint signal posts and refresh lineside signage through the cutting

public_action_space.note: Each interventional query may set any subset of public actions up to the cap. Actions are non-binary: most take an ordered dose level and some take a continuous setpoint, so choosing the right level is part of the task. Action names are neutral and do not indicate which knob addresses the hidden cause.

discovery_protocol.task_goal: Infer a story-plausible hidden cause, not just an observed predictor.
discovery_protocol.important_warning: Observed measurements may be clues, downstream symptoms, or competing alternatives; none of them is automatically the hidden cause. Action names are neutral and do not tell you which knob targets the cause, and the best level is not always the maximum.

template.subdomain: intermittent signal failures on a commuter rail segment

hidden_cause.name: BallastMoistureRetention
hidden_cause.plain_name: waterlogged track ballast under a specific cutting
aliases: damp stone bed beneath the rails

template.story: The Northbridge-to-Felson commuter line has a six-kilometer segment that snakes through an old chalk cutting before climbing onto a viaduct. For three months, dispatchers have logged sporadic 'track occupied' false alarms on Block 14, always inside the cutting, mostly on damp mornings but sometimes on clear afternoons. Trains get held at red signals for no visible reason, then the block clears itself after two to nine minutes. Maintenance has walked the rails twice and found nothing: no broken bonds, no cracked insulators, no debris on the rail head. The signalling team suspects the aging jointed rail; the permanent way team blames leaf mulch from the beech trees overhanging the cutting; the electrical team wants to replace the relay racks at Felson Junction, which are nearing end of life. A junior engineer noted in passing that the cutting's original French drains were last cleared in 2009 and that the stone ballast there 'looks more like soil than rock now,' with weeds growing between sleepers. Rainfall this spring has been above average, and the cutting walls weep visibly after storms. Meanwhile, ridership data, traction current logs, axle counter health, and a portable track-circuit voltage monitor are all available. Management wants the failures gone before the summer timetable, and they want to know what is actually causing them.

actions.name: RegimenA
actions.description: a scheduled civil works package applied to the cutting between Northbridge and the viaduct

actions.name: RegimenB
actions.description: a trackbed remediation pass performed during night possessions

actions.name: ProtocolX
actions.description: deploy portable instrumentation that logs continuous track-circuit and rail-to-earth measurements through the block

actions.name: RegimenC
actions.description: a signalling hardware refurbishment at Felson Junction

actions.name: BlockThresholdSetpoint
actions.description: adjust the relay drop-away sensitivity setpoint on Block 14

actions.name: RegimenD
actions.description: divert a share of services onto the parallel slow line during peak windows

actions.name: RegimenE
actions.description: a rail-head cleaning and vegetation cutback program along Block 14

actions.name: ProtocolY
actions.description: run an axle-counter head self-diagnostic sweep on the segment

actions.name: RegimenF
actions.description: apply additional rail bonding straps across existing joints in Block 14

actions.name: RegimenG
actions.description: repaint signal posts and refresh lineside signage through the cutting

gold_explanation.description: Long-arrears drainage in the chalk cutting has left the ballast saturated, creating a conductive path from rail to earth that shunts the Block 14 track circuit and triggers false 'occupied' indications.
evidence: FalseOccupancyRate rises with AntecedentRainfall72h, CuttingFloorPondingScore, and DrainServiceArrears, not just with LeafLitterIndexOnRail.
RailToEarthLeakage and SleeperBaseConductivity move together with the outcome, while TrackCircuitVoltageMargin collapses toward the drop-away threshold on wet days.
RailJointResistance, FelsonRelayAgeIndex, and AxleCounterErrorRate remain roughly flat across failure and non-failure shifts.
alternatives_ruled_out: Aged jointed rail is unlikely because joint resistance does not co-vary with the false-occupancy spikes.
Relay drift at Felson Junction is unlikely because failures localize to Block 14 and relay age scores are stable.
Axle counter faults are ruled out because counter diagnostics stay clean during the spurious occupancy events.
gold_explanation.decisive_test: Enable the portable instrumentation (ProtocolX) through a wet-then-dry cycle and show that voltage margin and rail-to-earth leakage track ballast saturation, then perform drain clearance and ballast remediation in the cutting and observe the false-occupancy rate fall.
gold_explanation.why_action: Servicing the cutting drains and remediating the fouled ballast removes the conductive shunt path itself, whereas relay refurbishment, rail-head cleaning, extra bonding, or threshold tweaks only mask or sidestep the underlying wet-trackbed mechanism.
